ontrack.label: ON TRACK · weekly check-in call
ontrack.framing: Phone call. Priya leads, Daniel is engaged and confident. Light-touch, surfaces a stretch goal.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Hi Daniel, it's Priya. Just my Tuesday check-in, are you alright to talk for two minutes?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah, fine. Just on my way back from the gym.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Brilliant. Right, your numbers are properly strong. Top quartile in your cohort. CV is signed off, you've saved nine vacancies, and the Level 2 course is locked in for the first of July.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah it's been alright, actually. I feel like I'm getting somewhere.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: I want to push you a little. Sundown Logistics is your match-of-the-week, but there's a Senior Warehouse Operative slot at Bow Distribution. Three grand more, same commute. Want me to send it across?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Senior, though? I haven't done senior before.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: You have, you just didn't call it that. Two years team-leading at Hackney was supervising six people. I'll pull it out on the CV and send you the link tonight.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Alright. Yeah, send it. Worst case I learn something at the interview.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: That's exactly the right attitude. Same time next Tuesday, and well done this week Daniel, properly well done.

drifting.label: DRIFTING · in-person re-engagement
drifting.framing: In person at the Stratford office. Daniel is quieter than usual, transport money is tight, confidence dipped. Priya is warm but practical.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Daniel, thanks for coming in. Sit down, do you want a tea or anything?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Nah, I'm alright thanks. Sorry I've been a bit quiet on the texts.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: No need to apologise. The system flagged that your replies dropped off, and I'd rather get you in than guess what's going on. What's been happening?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Honestly, the bus fare to get here adds up. And I rewrote that CV three times and I'm not sure it sounds like me anymore.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Right. Two things, then. I'm putting twenty pounds on a travel voucher today, that covers a fortnight of buses, no questions, no paperwork.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Seriously? That'd actually help a lot.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Yes seriously. Second thing, we're going back to the original CV. Your words, your warehouse experience, no clever language. I'll do the tidy up myself, you sign it off. Twenty minutes now, before you leave.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah, alright. That sounds better than starting again.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: And I'm pausing the daily app notifications for a week. They were too much. We'll go back to one text a week from me, just the important stuff.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Thanks Priya. I was starting to think I was the problem.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: You are not the problem. The plan was a bit too busy, that's on me. Let's reset it together, right now.

atrisk.label: AT RISK · same-day call, warm referral
atrisk.framing: Phone call early afternoon. Daniel is overwhelmed, sleep has gone, money worry. Priya stays calm, slows down, warm-refers to Access to Work Mental Health.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Daniel, it's Priya. I know we weren't scheduled today but I wanted to hear your voice. Have you got a minute?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah. Sorry, I haven't been picking up much this week.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: It's alright. How are you actually doing, not the work bit, just generally?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Not great if I'm honest. I'm not sleeping. The interview stuff has been spinning in my head and I keep thinking I'm going to mess it up and end up back where I was.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Thank you for telling me. That takes guts to say out loud, and none of what you just described means you're failing. It means you're carrying a lot.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Feels like I'm letting everyone down though.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: You are not letting anyone down. Here's what we do today. First, I'm pausing the job-match notifications for two weeks. No more new ones popping up. We're not adding pressure.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Okay, yeah, that would help.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Second, there's a Maximus service called Access to Work Mental Health Support. It's free, confidential, and a specialist will talk you through the sleep and anxiety side, separately from me. I'd like to refer you today, with your permission.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: It's free, yeah? I haven't got money for therapy.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Completely free, run by the same team I'm in. Nine months of support if you want it, you can stop whenever. Shall I make the call this afternoon?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, do it. Thanks Priya.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Last thing. I'm coming off the auto-plan and writing you a smaller one. Three things this week, not ten. We slow it down, we keep the door open, and we keep going. Alright?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Alright. Thanks for ringing.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Always. I'll text you when the referral is in. Get some food and try to sleep tonight, Daniel. We've got this.
